title: 100 Common Greek Words Every Learner Needs
content_html:
100 Common Greek Words Every Learner Needs

Building your Greek vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-restaurant-vocabulary-dining-out/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-moving-in-together/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-honeymoon-travel-phrases/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-expressing-emotions-feelings-vocabulary/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-date-night-vocabulary/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-baby-pregnancy-vocabulary/">vocabulary is the foundation of meaningful communication with your partner. These 100 essential words will help you navigate daily conversations and express yourself in Greek.

💕

Phrase to Learn

Ναι / Όχι

Yes / No

[ NEH / OH-hee ]

The two most fundamental words you'll use every day in Greek.

Greetings and Basics (1-15)

Greek English Pronunciation
Γεια σου Hello/Hi YAH-soo
Καλημέρα Good morning kah-lee-MEH-rah
Καλησπέρα Good evening kah-lee-SPEH-rah
Καληνύχτα Good night kah-lee-NEEKH-tah
Αντίο Goodbye ahn-DEE-oh
Ευχαριστώ Thank you ef-hah-ree-STOH
Παρακαλώ Please/You're welcome pah-rah-kah-LOH
Συγγνώμη Sorry/Excuse me see-GHNO-mee
Ναι Yes NEH
Όχι No OH-hee
Ίσως Maybe EE-sohs
Εντάξει Okay en-DAH-ksee
Σωστά Correct/Right soh-STAH
Λάθος Wrong/Mistake LAH-thos
Φυσικά Of course fee-see-KAH
Τι κάνεις; How are you?

Pronunciation: tee KAH-nees

"Γεια σου! Τι κάνεις;"

🇬🇷

Greek Greetings/learn/en/el/greek-good-morning-goodnight-phrases/">Greetings

Greeks typically greet with "Καλημέρα" until around 1pm, then switch to "Καλησπέρα." "Γεια σου" works anytime and is casual and friendly.

Pronouns and Question Words (16-30)

Greek English Pronunciation
Εγώ I eh-GHOH
Εσύ You eh-SEE
Αυτός He af-TOS
Αυτή She af-TEE
Εμείς We eh-MEES
Αυτοί/Αυτές They af-TEE / af-TES
Τι; What? tee
Ποιος/Ποια; Who? PYOS / PYA
Πού; Where? poo
Πότε; When? POH-teh
Γιατί; Why? yah-TEE
Πώς; How? pohs
Πόσο; How much? POH-soh
Ποιο; Which? PYO
Αυτό This af-TOH
Πού είσαι; Where are you?

Pronunciation: poo EE-seh

"Αγάπη μου, πού είσαι;"

Essential Verbs (31-50)

Greek English Pronunciation
Είμαι I am EE-meh
Έχω I have EH-ho
Θέλω I want THEH-loh
Μπορώ I can boh-ROH
Ξέρω I know KSEH-roh
Βλέπω I see VLEH-poh
Ακούω I hear ah-KOO-oh
Μιλάω I speak mee-LAH-oh
Καταλαβαίνω I understand kah-tah-lah-VEH-noh
Αγαπώ I love ah-gah-POH
Πάω I go PAH-oh
Έρχομαι I come EHR-hoh-meh
Κάνω I do/make KAH-noh
Τρώω I eat TROH-oh
Πίνω I drink PEE-noh
Δουλεύω I work thoo-LEH-voh
Μένω I live/stay MEH-noh
Περιμένω I wait peh-ree-MEH-noh
Χρειάζομαι I need hree-AH-zoh-meh
Νομίζω I think noh-MEE-zoh
Σ'αγαπώ I love you

Pronunciation: sah-gah-POH

"Σ'αγαπώ πολύ, καρδιά μου."

Time Words (51-65)

Greek English Pronunciation
Σήμερα Today SEE-meh-rah
Αύριο Tomorrow AHV-ree-oh
Χθες Yesterday hthes
Τώρα Now TOH-rah
Αργότερα Later ar-GOH-teh-rah
Πάντα Always PAHN-dah
Ποτέ Never poh-TEH
Μερικές φορές Sometimes meh-ree-KES foh-RES
Νωρίς Early noh-REES
Αργά Late ar-GAH
Πρωί Morning proh-EE
Μεσημέρι Noon meh-see-MEH-ree
Απόγευμα Afternoon ah-POH-yev-mah
Βράδυ Evening/Night VRAH-thee
Εβδομάδα Week ev-thoh-MAH-thah
Τα λέμε αύριο See you tomorrow

Pronunciation: tah LEH-meh AHV-ree-oh

"Καληνύχτα! Τα λέμε αύριο."

Numbers (66-75)

Greek English Pronunciation
Ένα One EH-nah
Δύο Two THEE-oh
Τρία Three TREE-ah
Τέσσερα Four TEH-seh-rah
Πέντε Five PEN-deh
Έξι Six EH-ksee
Επτά Seven ep-TAH
Οκτώ Eight ok-TOH
Εννέα Nine eh-NEH-ah
Δέκα Ten THEH-kah

Common Adjectives (76-90)

Greek English Pronunciation
Καλός/Καλή Good kah-LOS / kah-LEE
Κακός/Κακή Bad kah-KOS / kah-KEE
Μεγάλος Big meh-GAH-los
Μικρός Small mee-KROS
Όμορφος/Όμορφη Beautiful OH-mor-fos / OH-mor-fee
Ωραίος/Ωραία Nice/Pretty oh-REH-os / oh-REH-ah
Νέος/Νέα New/Young NEH-os / NEH-ah
Παλιός/Παλιά Old pah-LYOS / pah-LYAH
Εύκολο Easy EF-koh-loh
Δύσκολο Difficult THEES-koh-loh
Γρήγορα Fast/Quickly GHREE-gho-rah
Αργά Slowly ar-GAH
Κοντά Near kohn-DAH
Μακριά Far mah-kree-AH
Πολύ Very/Much poh-LEE
Είσαι όμορφη You are beautiful

Pronunciation: EE-seh OH-mor-fee

"Αγάπη μου, είσαι τόσο όμορφη."

🇬🇷

Greek Adjective Agreement

Greek adjectives change based on gender. Most adjectives have masculine (-ος), feminine (-η or -α), and neuter (-ο) forms. Match the adjective to the noun you're describing.

Everyday Nouns (91-100)

Greek English Pronunciation
Σπίτι House/Home SPEE-tee
Νερό Water neh-ROH
Φαγητό Food fah-yee-TOH
Καφές Coffee kah-FES
Δουλειά Work thoo-LYAH
Αγάπη Love ah-GAH-pee
Φίλος/Φίλη Friend FEE-los / FEE-lee
Οικογένεια Family ee-koh-YEH-nee-ah
Χρόνος Time/Year HROH-nos
Μέρα Day MEH-rah
Πάμε σπίτι Let's go home

Pronunciation: PAH-meh SPEE-tee

"Κουράστηκα. Πάμε σπίτι;"

Bonus: Essential Phrasesn/el/essential-greek-phrases-for-couples/">Essential Phrases

Δεν καταλαβαίνω I don't understand

Pronunciation: then kah-tah-lah-VEH-noh

"Συγγνώμη, δεν καταλαβαίνω."

Μιλάς αγγλικά; Do you speak English?

Pronunciation: mee-LAHS ahng-lee-KAH

"Συγγνώμη, μιλάς αγγλικά;"

Πώς το λένε αυτό; What is this called?

Pronunciation: pohs toh LEH-neh af-TOH

"Πώς το λένε αυτό στα ελληνικά;"

Study Tips

  1. Learn in pairs - Study with your partner for accountability
  2. Start with greetings - Use καλημέρα and καληνύχτα daily
  3. Label your home - Put Greek labels on items around the house
  4. Practice verbs first - Verbs are the backbone of conversation
  5. Use numbers daily - Count things in Greek throughout your day

These 100 words form the foundation of Greek fluency. Practice them regularly with your partner, and you'll be having basic conversations in no time!

title: Greek Forgiveness Phrases for English Speakers
content_html:
Forgiveness is essential for lasting love. When you're with a Greek speaker, knowing how to ask for and grant forgiveness in their language makes healing more meaningful. Language is a bridge to the heart, and in Greek culture, where emotions are often expressed with depth and intensity, using the right words during a conflict can significantly alter the path to reconciliation.

In a relationship where one partner speaks Greek, the act of apologizing in their native tongue demonstrates a profound level of commitment. It shows that you are willing to step out of your linguistic comfort zone to meet them where they are emotionally. This effort speaks volumes, often communicating sincerity even before the full meaning of the sentence is processed.

Asking for Forgiveness

When seeking reconciliation in a Greek context, the words you choose carry the weight of your intentions. Greek culture places a high value on "filotimo"—a complex concept involving honor, respect, and doing what is right for the relationship. Consequently, asking for forgiveness is seen as an act of restoring the balance and harmony that may have been disrupted by a misunderstanding or a mistake.

In this section, we will explore the most direct and powerful way to ask for this grace. We will focus on the imperative form used between partners, which strips away clinical distance and addresses the person directly. Understanding the nuance of this request helps ensure that your apology is received as a genuine olive branch rather than a mere social formality.

We will also look at how tone and timing play into this request. In Greek communication, the energy behind the words is just as vital as the vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-restaurant-vocabulary-dining-out/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-moving-in-together/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-honeymoon-travel-phrases/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-expressing-emotions-feelings-vocabulary/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-date-night-vocabulary/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-baby-pregnancy-vocabulary/">vocabulary itself. By using the specific phrase outlined below, you are signaling a desire to close the gap that hurt has created between you and your partner.

Συγγνώμη Sorry / Excuse me

Pronunciation: see-GHNO-mee

"Συγγνώμη, μπορώ να περάσω;"

Συγχώρεσέ με

Pronunciation: seen-KHOH-reh-seh meh

Meaning: "Forgive me"

When to use it: A direct, heartfelt request for forgiveness. This phrase shows humility and genuine regret. It is more substantial than a simple "sorry" and is typically reserved for moments where you recognize that a deeper wound needs mending. Using this phrase indicates that you are seeking more than just an acknowledgment of a mistake; you are seeking a restoration of the bond.

Granting Forgiveness

Accepting an apology and granting forgiveness is a transformative moment in any partnership. In the Greek linguistic tradition, the concept of forgiveness is closely linked to the idea of "making room" or "fitting together" again. It is a conscious decision to move past the "pikra" (bitterness) that may have accumulated during a period of tension.

This section covers the primary phrase used to signal that the emotional debt of a mistake has been cleared. We will examine how to state this clearly so that both partners can feel the weight of the conflict lift. Granting forgiveness is not a passive act; it is an active contribution to the health of the relationship.

By learning how to verbally grant forgiveness, you provide your partner with the closure they need to stop feeling guilty. It marks the definitive end of a cycle of hurt and the beginning of a new chapter of understanding. Below, we focus on the phrase that seals this agreement of peace.

Το ξεχνάμε We forget it

Pronunciation: toh kseh-KHNA-meh

"Δεν πειράζει, το ξεχνάμε."

Σε συγχωρώ

Pronunciation: seh seen-khoh-ROH

Meaning: "I forgive you"

When to use it: To grant forgiveness directly and sincerely, releasing resentment and choosing to move forward. This phrase is a powerful tool for emotional release. It tells your partner that you have processed the hurt and are no longer holding the grievance against them. It is a verbal contract of emotional freedom.

The Process of Forgiveness

Asking for forgiveness requires vulnerability. Express that you understand the pain you caused, take full responsibility without making excuses, and show genuine remorse for your actions. In a Greek-speaking household, this often involves a face-to-face conversation where eye contact and physical presence are maintained. Avoiding the issue or offering a flippant text message apology usually falls short of the cultural expectation for emotional honesty.

Granting forgiveness is equally meaningful. Let your partner know you're choosing to release anger and resentment. This doesn't mean forgetting—it means choosing your relationship over holding onto hurt. The process involves a transition from the "εγώ" (I) to the "εμείς" (us), where the collective well-being of the couple takes precedence over individual pride.

Sometimes forgiveness takes time. It's okay to say you want to forgive but need space to process. Honor your feelings while working toward healing. In Greek, this period of reflection is understood as a necessary step toward a "metanoia"—a change of heart or mind that leads to a better future.

Moving Forward Together

After forgiveness, focus on rebuilding trust and preventing similar hurts. Discuss what went wrong and how you can both do better. This is the time for "kouvenda"—meaningful talk—where the underlying causes of the conflict are gently explored without the heat of the initial argument.

The concept of "Συγχώρεση" in Greek represents this healing power. Physical affection, quality time, and renewed commitment help restore your bond. Often, a shared meal or a long walk can act as the secondary ritual that solidifies the verbal forgiveness you have exchanged.

Trust is rebuilt through consistent actions that align with your apologies. In a cross-cultural relationship, this might mean being more mindful of specific cultural triggers or communication styles that led to the friction in the first place.

Cultural Considerations

Greek speakers often value sincere emotional expression. A heartfelt request for forgiveness shows respect for your partner and the relationship. The culture tends to be "high-context," meaning that the environment, your body language, and your history together all inform how your words are interpreted.

After reconciliation, warmth and closeness help heal any remaining wounds. It is common for Greek couples to follow a difficult/learn/en/el/greek-support-phrases-for-english-speakers/">difficult conversation with a display of affection or a return to the playful "piragma" (teasing) that characterizes many Greek interactions. This return to normalcy is a sign that the forgiveness was successful and the relationship is once again on solid ground. Embracing these cultural rhythms helps ensure that forgiveness isn't just a word spoken, but a state of being lived.

title: Greek for Couples: 50+ Essential Phrases
content_html:
Learning Greek for your partner is one of the most romantic things you can do. This guide covers all the essential phrases you'll need for daily communication, romantic moments, and building a deeper connection with your Greek-speaking loved one.

The Most Important Phrase

💕

Phrase to Learn

Σ'αγαπώ

I love you

[ sah-gah-POH ]

The foundation of romantic Greek - say it often and mean it.

Morning and Evening Greetings/learn/en/el/greek-good-morning-goodnight-phrases/">Greetings

Start and end each day with these warm Greek greetings:

Καλημέρα, αγάπη μου Good morning, my love

Pronunciation: kah-lee-MEH-rah, ah-GAH-pee moo

"Καλημέρα, αγάπη μου. Κοιμήθηκες καλά το βράδυ;"

Καλησπέρα, καρδιά μου Good evening, my heart

Pronunciation: kah-lee-SPEH-rah, kar-THYAH moo

"Καλησπέρα, καρδιά μου. Πώς ήταν η μέρα σου σήμερα;"

Καληνύχτα, γλυκιά μου Goodnight, my sweet (f)

Pronunciation: kah-lee-NEEKH-tah, ghlee-KYAH moo

"Καληνύχτα, γλυκιά μου. Όνειρα γλυκά!"

Όνειρα γλυκά Sweet dreams

Pronunciation: OH-nee-rah ghlee-KAH

"Κοιμήσου καλά. Όνειρα γλυκά!"

🇬🇷

Greek Greeting Culture

Greeks take greetings seriously! A warm "καλημέρα" (good morning) or "καλησπέρα" (good evening) is expected and appreciated. Skipping greetings can seem cold. When greeting your partner's family, always greet each person individually.

Asking How Your Partner Is

Πώς είσαι, αγάπη μου; How are you, my love?

Pronunciation: pos EE-seh, ah-GAH-pee moo?

"Καλημέρα! Πώς είσαι σήμερα το πρωί, αγάπη μου;"

Τι κάνεις, ζωή μου; How are you doing, my life?

Pronunciation: tee KAH-nees, zoh-EE moo?

"Γεια σου, ζωή μου. Τι κάνεις;"

Είμαι καλά, ευχαριστώ I'm good/fine, thank you

Pronunciation: EE-meh kah-LAH, ef-hah-ree-STOH

"Είμαι καλά, ευχαριστώ. Εσύ πώς είσαι;"

Πώς ήταν η μέρα σου, αγάπη μου; How was your day, my love?

Pronunciation: pos EE-tan ee MEH-rah soo, ah-GAH-pee moo?

"Πώς ήταν η μέρα σου στη δουλειά, αγάπη μου;"

Expressing Love and Affection

Σ'αγαπώ πολύ I love you very much

Pronunciation: sah-gah-POH poh-LEE

"Σ'αγαπώ πολύ, ζωή μου, το ξέρεις."

Μου έλειψες I missed you

Pronunciation: moo EH-lee-pses

"Μου έλειψες πολύ σήμερα, ανυπομονούσα να σε δω."

Μου λείπεις I miss you

Pronunciation: moo LEE-pees

"Μου λείπεις όταν δεν είσαι δίπλα μου."

Σκέφτομαι εσένα I'm thinking of you

Pronunciation: SKEF-toh-meh eh-SEH-nah

"Σκέφτομαι εσένα συνέχεια, αγάπη μου."

Είσαι τα πάντα μου You are my everything

Pronunciation: EE-seh tah PAHN-dah moo

"Είσαι τα πάντα μου, αγάπη μου, δεν μπορώ χωρίς εσένα."

Giving Compliments

Greeks love giving and receiving compliments. Use these to make your partner feel special:

Είσαι πανέμορφη / πανέμορφος You are gorgeous (f/m)

Pronunciation: EE-seh pah-NEH-mor-fee / pah-NEH-mor-fos

"Είσαι πανέμορφη απόψε με αυτό το φόρεμα!"

Είσαι όμορφη / όμορφος You are beautiful/handsome (f/m)

Pronunciation: EE-seh OH-mor-fee / OH-mor-fos

"Είσαι τόσο όμορφος σήμερα."

Σου πάει πολύ It suits you very well

Pronunciation: soo PA-ee poh-LEE

"Αυτό το χρώμα σου πάει πολύ."

Έχεις όμορφα μάτια You have beautiful eyes

Pronunciation: EH-hees OH-mor-fah MAH-tyah

"Έχεις τα πιο όμορφα μάτια που έχω δει."

Έχεις υπέροχο χαμόγελο You have a wonderful smile

Pronunciation: EH-hees ee-PEH-roh-hoh hah-MOH-gheh-loh

"Λατρεύω το χαμόγελό σου. Έχεις ένα υπέροχο χαμόγελο."

🇬🇷

Greek Expressiveness

Greeks tend to be more expressive with compliments than Northern Europeans or Americans. Don't hold back - if you think your partner looks beautiful, tell them! Enthusiastic compliments are appreciated and expected in Greek romantic relationships.

Making Plans Together

Θέλεις να βγούμε απόψε; Do you want to go out tonight?

Pronunciation: THEH-lees nah VGHOO-meh ah-POH-pseh?

"Θέλεις να βγούμε απόψε για ένα ποτό;"

Πάμε για φαγητό; Shall we go for food?

Pronunciation: PAH-meh yah fah-yee-TOH?

"Πεινάω! Πάμε για φαγητό;"

Τι θα ήθελες να κάνουμε σήμερα; What would you like for us to do today?

Pronunciation: tee thah EE-theh-les nah KAH-noo-meh SEE-meh-rah?

"Τι θα ήθελες να κάνουμε το Σαββατοκύριακο μαζί;"

Πάμε βόλτα; Shall we go for a walk?

Pronunciation: PAH-meh VOHL-tah?

"Ο καιρός είναι ωραίος. Πάμε βόλτα στην παραλία;"

Θα σε πάρω τηλέφωνο I'll call you

Pronunciation: thah seh PAH-roh tee-LEH-foh-noh

"Πρέπει να φύγω τώρα. Θα σε πάρω τηλέφωνο αργότερα."

Expressing Appreciation

Ευχαριστώ, αγάπη μου Thank you, my love

Pronunciation: ef-hah-ree-STOH, ah-GAH-pee moo

"Ευχαριστώ για το υπέροχο δείπνο, αγάπη μου."

Σ'ευχαριστώ για όλα Thank you for everything

Pronunciation: sef-hah-ree-STOH yah OH-lah

"Σ'ευχαριστώ για όλα, καρδιά μου, είσαι μοναδική."

Είσαι ο καλύτερος / η καλύτερη You're the best (m/f)

Pronunciation: EE-seh oh kah-LEE-teh-ros / ee kah-LEE-teh-ree

"Είσαι η καλύτερη, αγάπη μου! Σε ευχαριστώ!"

Τι θα έκανα χωρίς εσένα; What would I do without you?

Pronunciation: tee thah EH-kah-nah hoh-REES eh-SEH-nah?

"Είμαι τόσο τυχερή που σε έχω. Τι θα έκανα χωρίς εσένα;"

Physical Affection

Έλα να σε αγκαλιάσω Come, let me hug you

Pronunciation: EH-lah nah seh an-gah-LYAH-soh

"Μου έλειψες πολύ. Έλα εδώ να σε αγκαλιάσω."

Δώσε μου ένα φιλί Give me a kiss

Pronunciation: THOH-seh moo EH-nah fee-LEE

"Δώσε μου ένα φιλί πριν φύγεις για τη δουλειά."

Θέλω να σε αγκαλιάσω I want to hug you

Pronunciation: THEH-loh nah seh an-gah-LYAH-soh

"Μετά από μια δύσκολη μέρα, θέλω να σε αγκαλιάσω."

Κράτα με Hold me

Pronunciation: KRAH-tah meh

"Κράτα με σφιχτά, νιώθω ασφάλεια κοντά σου."

Apologizing and Making Up

Συγγνώμη, αγάπη μου I'm sorry, my love

Pronunciation: see-GHNO-mee, ah-GAH-pee moo

"Συγγνώμη, αγάπη μου. Έκανα ένα λάθος."

Με συγχωρείς; Will you forgive me?

Pronunciation: meh seen-hoh-REES?

"Με συγχωρείς; Δεν το ήθελα να σε πληγώσω."

Λυπάμαι πολύ I'm very sorry

Pronunciation: lee-PAH-meh poh-LEE

"Λυπάμαι πολύ που σε στεναχώρησα τόσο."

Δεν θα ξανασυμβεί It won't happen again

Pronunciation: then thah xah-nah-seem-VEE

"Συγγνώμη. Σου υπόσχομαι, δεν θα ξανασυμβεί."

Σε συγχωρώ I forgive you

Pronunciation: seh seen-hoh-ROH

"Σε συγχωρώ, αγάπη μου, όλα είναι καλά."

🇬🇷

Greek Reconciliation

Greeks can be passionate during disagreements, but they also believe strongly in reconciliation. A sincere apology followed by a warm embrace is the Greek way. Don't let small arguments linger - address them and move forward with love.

Important Questions

Με αγαπάς; Do you love me?

Pronunciation: meh ah-gah-PAS?

"Μωρό μου, με αγαπάς ακόμα;"

Θα είσαι πάντα μαζί μου; Will you always be with me?

Pronunciation: thah EE-seh PAHN-dah mah-ZEE moo?

"Θα είσαι πάντα μαζί μου, αγάπη μου, σε όλα;"

Τι σκέφτεσαι; What are you thinking?

Pronunciation: tee SKEF-teh-seh?

"Είσαι σιωπηλός. Τι σκέφτεσαι, αγάπη μου;"

Daily Essentials

Να προσέχεις Take care / Be careful

Pronunciation: nah proh-SEH-hees

"Να προσέχεις στη δουλειά σήμερα!"

Περαστικά Get well soon

Pronunciation: peh-rah-stee-KAH

"Περαστικά, αγάπη μου. Νιώθεις καλύτερα τώρα;"

Καλή επιτυχία Good luck

Pronunciation: kah-LEE eh-pee-tee-HEE-ah

"Καλή επιτυχία στην παρουσίαση αύριο!"

Θα τα πούμε αργότερα We'll talk later

Pronunciation: thah tah POO-meh ar-GHOH-teh-rah

"Πρέπει να τρέξω. Θα τα πούμε αργότερα, φιλιά!"

Quick Reference Card

Situation Greek English
Morning Καλημέρα, αγάπη μου Good morning, my love
Evening Καλησπέρα, καρδιά μου Good evening, my heart
Bedtime Καληνύχτα, γλυκιά μου Goodnight, my sweet
Leaving Σ'αγαπώ, τα λέμε I love you, talk soon
Missing them Μου λείπεις πολύ I miss you so much
Grateful Σ'ευχαριστώ για όλα Thank you for everything
Apologizing Συγγνώμη, αγάπη μου Sorry, my love

These essential phrases will help you navigate daily life and romantic moments with your Greek partner. The key is to use them regularly and naturally. Start with the greetings and love expressions, then gradually add more as you become comfortable.

Remember: Greeks appreciate effort and emotion. Even imperfect Greek spoken with love will touch your partner's heart more than silence ever could.

title: Greek Alphabet Guide for Couples
content_html:
Learning the Greek alphabet together as a couple is one of the most rewarding first steps in your language journey. While the script may look unfamiliar at first, you will be surprised how quickly you can master it when you practice together. The Greek alphabet has only 24 letters, and many of them will feel familiar once you understand the sounds they represent.

This guide will take you through every letter, with pronunciation tips, romantic vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-restaurant-vocabulary-dining-out/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-moving-in-together/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-honeymoon-travel-phrases/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-expressing-emotions-feelings-vocabulary/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-date-night-vocabulary/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-baby-pregnancy-vocabulary/">vocabulary to practice, and exercises designed for couples learning together.

Why Learn the Greek Alphabet First?

Before diving in, understand why mastering the Greek alphabet is essential:

  1. Greek uses a unique script - Unlike Spanish or French, you cannot simply read Greek using English letters
  2. Transliteration is inconsistent - Different sources spell Greek words differently in English letters
  3. It unlocks authentic learning - Reading Greek script opens doors to signs, menus, and love notes from your partner
  4. It is easier than you think - Many Greek letters look or sound similar to English ones
🇬🇷

The Birthplace of the Alphabet

The Greek alphabet, developed around 800 BCE, was the first to include vowels and is the ancestor of the Latin alphabet we use in English. When you learn Greek letters, you are connecting with over 2,800 years of written history.

The Greek Alphabet: All 24 Letters

Let us break down the alphabet into manageable groups. Practice each group together before moving on.

Group 1: Familiar Friends (Letters That Look and Sound Similar to English)

These letters will feel comfortable right away:

Greek Name Pronunciation English Equivalent
A α Alpha AHL-fah A as in "father"
E ε Epsilon EHP-see-lon E as in "pet"
I ι Iota YOH-tah I as in "machine"
K κ Kappa KAH-pah K as in "key"
M μ Mi mee M as in "mother"
N ν Ni nee N as in "no"
O ο Omicron OH-mee-kron O as in "hot"
T τ Tau taf T as in "top"
Z ζ Zeta ZEE-tah Z as in "zoo"
αγαπημένος / αγαπημένη beloved (m/f)

Pronunciation: ah-gah-pee-MEH-nohs / ah-gah-pee-MEH-nee

"Είσαι ο αγαπημένος/η μου."

βέρα wedding ring

Pronunciation: VEH-rah

"Η βέρα συμβολίζει την αιώνια αγάπη μας."

Group 2: New Sounds, Familiar Shapes

These letters look different but represent sounds you know:

Greek Name Pronunciation Sound
Β β Vita VEE-tah V as in "very" (not B!)
Γ γ Gamma GHAH-mah Soft G, like "y" in "yes" before e/i sounds
Δ δ Delta THEL-tah TH as in "this" (voiced)
Η η Ita EE-tah EE as in "see"
Λ λ Lambda LAHM-thah L as in "love"
Π π Pi pee P as in "pen"
Ρ ρ Rho roh Rolled R (like Spanish)
Σ σ/ς Sigma SEEG-mah S as in "sun"
γάμος marriage

Pronunciation: GAH-mos

"Ο γάμος μας ήταν υπέροχος."

δέσμευση commitment

Pronunciation: thes-MEV-see

"Η δέσμευση μας είναι ισχυρή."

🇬🇷

The Two Sigmas

Greek has two forms of the lowercase sigma: σ is used at the beginning or middle of words, while ς is used only at the end. For example: σπίτι (house) ends with ι, but Κώστας (Kostas, a name) ends with ς.

Group 3: Unique Greek Sounds

These letters represent sounds that may be new to you:

Greek Name Pronunciation Sound
Θ θ Thita THEE-tah TH as in "think" (unvoiced)
Ξ ξ Xi ksee KS as in "box"
Φ φ Fi fee F as in "fun"
Χ χ Chi hee CH as in Scottish "loch"
Ψ ψ Psi psee PS as in "oops"
Ω ω Omega oh-MEH-ghah O as in "go" (same as omicron in modern Greek)
έρωτας passionate love / eros

Pronunciation: EH-roh-tas

"Ο έρωτάς μας είναι μοναδικός."

ζωή life / our life together

Pronunciation: zoh-EE

"Θέλω να περάσω όλη μου τη ζωή μαζί σου."

Group 4: The Tricky Ones

These letters often confuse English speakers because they look like English letters but sound different:

Greek Name Looks Like Actually Sounds Like
Υ υ Ypsilon Y/U EE as in "see"
Ρ ρ Rho P Rolled R
Ν ν Ni V N as in "no"
Η η Ita H EE as in "see"
Χ χ Chi X CH as in "loch"
💕

Phrase to Learn

Είμαι ερωτευμένος/η μαζί σου

I am in love with you (m/f)

[ EE-meh eh-roh-tev-MEH-nohs / eh-roh-tev-MEH-nee mah-ZEE soo ]

The most important Greek phrase - now you can read it in Greek script!

Common Letter Combinations

Greek has several letter combinations that create distinct sounds. Master these to read fluently:

Combination Sound Example
ου "oo" as in "moon" μου (moo) - my
αι "e" as in "pet" και (keh) - and
ει "ee" as in "see" είσαι (EE-seh) - you are
οι "ee" as in "see" μοιράζομαι (mee-RAH-zoh-meh) - I share
μπ "b" as in "boy" μπαμπάς (bah-BAHS) - dad
ντ "d" as in "dog" ντομάτα (doh-MAH-tah) - tomato
γκ/γγ "g" as in "go" αγγλικά (ang-lee-KAH) - English
τσ "ts" as in "cats" τσάι (TSAH-ee) - tea
τζ "dz" as in "adze" τζατζίκι (dzah-DZEE-kee) - tzatziki
ηρεμία serenity / calm

Pronunciation: ee-reh-MEE-ah

"Η παρουσία σου φέρνει ηρεμία."

θάλπος warmth / affection

Pronunciation: THAL-pos

"Το θάλπος της αγκαλιάς σου με γεμίζει."

Romantic Alphabet Practice for Couples

Now let us put your alphabet knowledge to work with romantic vocabulary:

Love Words A to O

ισορροπία balance

Pronunciation: ee-so-roh-PEE-ah

"Η ισορροπία στη σχέση μας είναι το κλειδί."

λάμψη glow / spark

Pronunciation: LAHM-psee

"Η λάμψη στα μάτια σου με συνεπαίρνει."

μέλλον future

Pronunciation: MEL-lon

"Χτίζουμε ένα κοινό μέλλον μαζί."

Love Words P to Omega

νάζι coyness / flirtatious charm

Pronunciation: NAH-zee

"Το νάζι σου είναι ακαταμάχητο."

φλογερός / φλογερή passionate / fiery (m/f)

Pronunciation: flo-gheh-ROS / flo-gheh-REE

"Η αγάπη μας είναι φλογερή."

Practice Exercises for Couples

Integrating a new script into your daily life is more effective when you move beyond passive observation. By working through these targeted exercises, you can solidify your understanding of the Greek alphabet’s phonetic rules and visual nuances. These activities encourage you to decode symbols in real-time and use them to express sentiment, which helps bridge the gap between abstract learning and practical speech.

The following exercises are designed to progress from recognition to production. You will start with a Letter Recognition Game to build speed, then move into reading specific love words to master vowel blends and diphthongs. Afterward, the focus shifts to writing small notes to internalize Greek orthography, followed by a Daily Alphabet Challenge that establishes a consistent rhythm for your learning journey together.

Exercise 1: Letter Recognition Game

Take turns pointing to random Greek letters and having your partner name them. Keep score and celebrate victories with a kiss!

Exercise 2: Read These Love Words Together

Practice reading these romantic words aloud:

  1. αγάπη (love)
  2. καρδιά (heart)
  3. φιλί (kiss)
  4. μαζί (together)
  5. πάντα (always)

Exercise 3: Write Love Notes

Try writing simple messages to each other using the Greek alphabet:

  • Σ'αγαπώ (I love you)
  • Μου λείπεις (I miss you)
  • Είσαι υπέροχη/υπέροχος (You are wonderful)

Exercise 4: Daily Alphabet Challenge

Each day, learn one new Greek word together that starts with the next letter of the alphabet. By the end of a month, you will have built a solid vocabulary foundation!

🇬🇷

Greek Letters in Science and Math

You already know some Greek letters! Alpha (α), beta (β), gamma (γ), delta (Δ), pi (π), and omega (Ω) are used in mathematics and science. This prior familiarity will help you learn faster.

Quick Reference: The Complete Greek Alphabet

# Upper Lower Name Sound
1 Α α Alpha ah
2 Β β Vita v
3 Γ γ Gamma gh/y
4 Δ δ Delta th (voiced)
5 Ε ε Epsilon eh
6 Ζ ζ Zeta z
7 Η η Ita ee
8 Θ θ Thita th (unvoiced)
9 Ι ι Iota ee
10 Κ κ Kappa k
11 Λ λ Lambda l
12 Μ μ Mi m
13 Ν ν Ni n
14 Ξ ξ Xi ks
15 Ο ο Omicron o
16 Π π Pi p
17 Ρ ρ Rho r (rolled)
18 Σ σ/ς Sigma s
19 Τ τ Tau t
20 Υ υ Ypsilon ee
21 Φ φ Fi f
22 Χ χ Chi ch (as in loch)
23 Ψ ψ Psi ps
24 Ω ω Omega o

Tips for Success

  1. Practice daily - Even 10 minutes a day together makes a difference
  2. Write by hand - Writing the letters helps cement them in memory
  3. Read everything - Try to read Greek signs, menus, and social media posts
  4. Be patient - The alphabet takes most couples 2-3 weeks to master
  5. Make it romantic - Write love notes to each other in Greek script

Mastering the Greek alphabet together is a beautiful milestone in your language learning journey. Once you can read Greek script, a whole new world opens up - from reading love poems to understanding the signs in Greece to writing secret messages to each other.

Take your time, support each other, and celebrate every small victory. Before you know it, you will be reading Σ'αγαπώ as naturally as "I love you."

title: Greek Anniversary Wishes: Romantic Messages for Your Love
content_html:
Celebrating your επέτειος in Greek adds a beautiful layer of meaning to this special occasion. Whether you're marking your first year μαζί or your golden επέτειος, expressing your αγάπη in your partner's language shows thoughtfulness and dedication to your relationship.

For everyday romantic expressions, check out our guide on Greek romantic phrases for every occasion.

💕

Phrase to Learn

χρόνια πολλά

Happy Anniversary

[ KHRO-nyah po-LAH ]

The perfect way to greet your partner on your special day.

Essential Anniversary Vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-moving-in-together/">Vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-honeymoon-travel-phrases/">Vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-expressing-emotions-feelings-vocabulary/">Vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-date-night-vocabulary/">Vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-baby-pregnancy-vocabulary/">Vocabulary

επέτειος anniversary

Pronunciation: eh-PEH-tee-oss

"Σήμερα είναι η επέτειός μας!"

Greek English Usage
επέτειος anniversary The special date
χρόνια years Time together
μαζί together United as a couple
πάντα forever Eternal love
αγάπη love Your bond
γιορτάζω celebrate To honor the day
ανάμνηση memory Cherished moments
ορόσημο milestone Important markers
🇬🇷

Greek Anniversary Traditions

In Greek-speaking cultures, anniversaries are often celebrated with romantic dinners, meaningful gifts, and sometimes renewing vows. Each milestone year may have traditional gift themes associated with it.

Simple Anniversary Wishes

Greek anniversary greetings/learn/en/el/greek-good-morning-goodnight-phrases/">greetings often rely on established formulas that emphasize longevity and health. While the phrase 'Xronia Polla' serves as a versatile greeting for many occasions, specific modifiers help tailor the message to a romantic milestone. This section focuses on clear, direct language that conveys warmth without becoming overly complex or flowery.

In the following subsections, you will find 'Short and Sweet' options ideal for social media captions or quick text messages. Additionally, the 'For Your Partner' category provides phrases that use the singular 'you' to create a more direct and personal connection between two people celebrating their shared history.

Short and Sweet

  • χρόνια πολλά! - Happy Anniversary!
  • Another beautiful year μαζί! - Celebrating time together
  • To many more χρόνια! - Looking forward

For Your Partner

  • You make every day special - Expressing gratitude
  • αγάπη you more each day - Growing love
  • My heart is πάντα yours - Eternal commitment
αγάπη love

Pronunciation: ah-GHA-pee

"Η αγάπη δυναμώνει κάθε χρόνο."

Romantic Anniversary Messages

The Greek language is renowned for its depth of emotion and poetic structure, making it an ideal medium for expressing romantic devotion. This section moves beyond basic greetings to explore more sophisticated ways of describing love and commitment. You will notice that many of these expressions use metaphors related to the soul, heart, and the concept of 'everlasting' connection.

We have categorized these into 'Heartfelt Declarations' for those moments when you want to express the intensity of your feelings, and 'Reflective Messages' which focus on the passage of time and the growth of the relationship. These phrases help articulate the evolution of a bond from a simple romance into a lifelong partnership.

Heartfelt Declarations

"Every επέτειος with you reminds me why I chose you. You are my πάντα."

"μαζί we've created a beautiful life. Here's to many more χρόνια of αγάπη."

Reflective Messages

"Looking back on our ανάμνηση fills my heart with joy. Thank you for being my everything."

"Each ορόσημο we reach together makes our bond stronger. I εκτιμώ every moment with you."

🇬🇷

Anniversary Milestone Names

Different επέτειος χρόνια have traditional names: first (paper), fifth (wood), tenth (tin), twenty-fifth (silver), and fiftieth (gold). These traditions are similar across many Greek-speaking regions.

Anniversary Messages by Year

Milestones in Greek culture are often celebrated with specific linguistic nuances that reflect the weight of the achievement. As a relationship matures, the language used to describe it often shifts from the excitement of 'newness' to a tone of profound respect and endurance. This section provides tailored messages for various stages of a journey together, ensuring the vocabulary matches the significance of the date.

From the fresh enthusiasm of a 'First Anniversary' to the foundational 'Fifth' and 'Tenth' markers, you will find phrases that suit the occasion. We also include traditional greetings for the 'Twenty-Fifth Anniversary (Silver)' and the 'Fiftieth Anniversary (Golden),' which are historically viewed as major family events in Greece, often involving larger gatherings and more formal declarations.

γάμος marriage

Pronunciation: ghá-mos

"Ο γάμος μας δυναμώνει με το χρόνο."

First Anniversary

"One year of αγάπη, laughter, and happiness. The first of many beautiful χρόνια μαζί!"

Fifth Anniversary

"Five χρόνια of making ανάμνηση together. You still give me butterflies."

Tenth Anniversary

"A decade of αγάπη! Every day with you is a gift I εκτιμώ."

Twenty-Fifth Anniversary (Silver)

"Twenty-five χρόνια of αγάπη and counting. You are my greatest ορόσημο."

Fiftieth Anniversary (Golden)

"Fifty χρόνια μαζί - a golden αγάπη story. You are my πάντα."

Romantic Phrases for Cards

Occasion Greek Message Meaning
Any year χρόνια πολλά my αγάπη Happy anniversary my love
First year Our first επέτειος! Celebrating year one
Special year ορόσημο επέτειος Major anniversary
Every year πάντα μαζί Eternal togetherness

Planning Your Anniversary Celebration

Celebrating an anniversary involves more than just selecting the right words; it requires the vocabulary to coordinate the event itself. In Greece, celebrations are frequently centered around shared meals and vibrant social environments. Understanding how to propose a date or suggest a specific activity using local terminology allows for a more immersive cultural experience during your milestone.

This section outlines 'Romantic Ideas' for planning your day and provides a guide on 'What to Say at Dinner' to help navigate the etiquette of Greek toasts and dinner conversations. Using these phrases helps set the mood and ensures that the logistical aspects of the evening are handled with the same romantic flair as your written messages.

γιορτάζω to celebrate

Pronunciation: yior-tá-zo

"Θέλω να γιορτάσουμε μαζί την επέτειό μας."

Romantic Ideas

  1. Write a card in Greek
  2. Learn a Greek love song
  3. Cook a traditional Greek dinner
  4. Plan a trip to a Greek-speaking destination
  5. Create a ανάμνηση book with Greek captions

What to Say at Dinner

  • "To us and our beautiful αγάπη!" - A perfect toast
  • "Here's to πάντα!" - Raising your glass
  • "Thank you for choosing me" - Expressing gratitude

For more terms of endearment, explore our Greek pet names guide.

Quick Reference Card

Purpose Say This
Greeting χρόνια πολλά!
Declaration I αγάπη you
Toast To πάντα!
Gratitude Thank you for every ανάμνηση

Every επέτειος is a chance to celebrate your αγάπη story. Speaking these words in Greek makes the moment even more special and shows your partner how much their language and culture mean to you.

title: Greek Arguing & Disagreement Phrases for English Speakers
content_html:
Greek culture embraces passionate expression—including during disagreements. When your partner speaks Greek, knowing how to express yourself during conflicts in their language shows deep commitment. Here are essential Greek phrases for navigating those heated moments.

Expressing Disagreement

In Greek culture, conversations can be passionate and high-energy, but clearly stating your position is essential for effective communication. To navigate these moments, you need phrases that allow you to stand your ground without necessarily ending the dialogue. Simply stating that you do not agree (Δεν συμφωνώ) provides a direct foundation, while pointing out a lack of fairness (Δεν είναι δίκαιο) shifts the focus to the ethics of the situation.

Often, disagreements arise from a breakdown in comprehension rather than a difference in values. If you feel your point is being misinterpreted, clarifying that you are being misunderstood (Με παρεξηγείς) can hit the reset button on the conversation. Similarly, if the other person is dominating the talk, reminding them that they aren't listening (Δεν με ακούς) highlights the need for a two-way exchange.

Συμφωνώ I agree

Pronunciation: sim-fo-NO

"Συμφωνώ με την άποψή σου για το θέμα."

Δεν συμφωνώ (Den simfonó)

Pronunciation: then seem-foh-NOH

Meaning: "I don't agree" or "I disagree"

When to use it: The direct way to express disagreement in Greek.

Example: "Δεν συμφωνώ με αυτό που είπες." (I don't agree with what you said.)

Δεν είναι δίκαιο (Den íne díkeo)

Pronunciation: then EE-neh THEE-keh-oh

Meaning: "That's not fair"

When to use it: When something feels inequitable in your relationship.

Με παρεξηγείς (Me parexigís)

Pronunciation: meh pah-reh-ksee-YEES

Meaning: "You're misunderstanding me"

When to use it: When your words or intentions are being misread.

Δεν με ακούς (Den me akoús)

Pronunciation: then meh ah-KOOS

Meaning: "You're not listening to me"

When to use it: When you feel unheard during an argument.

Expressing Frustration

When an argument shifts from a logical debate to an emotional one, communicating your internal state becomes the priority. Greek uses specific verb structures and adjectives to articulate these feelings clearly. Using the phrase "It bothers me when..." (Με ενοχλεί όταν...) allows you to identify specific triggers, while acknowledging deep disappointment (Είμαι απογοητευμένος/η) provides insight into your emotional stakes in the matter.

It is also helpful to signal when a conversation has reached a point of causing actual distress. Stating that a comment or action is hurtful (Αυτό με πληγώνει) moves the discussion away from accusations and toward vulnerability. If the intensity becomes too high to manage in the moment, requesting some time to process (Χρειάζομαι λίγο χρόνο) is a practical way to prevent the situation from escalating further.

Ενοχλώ To bother / To annoy

Pronunciation: e-no-HLO

"Με ενοχλεί η φασαρία όταν προσπαθώ να κοιμηθώ."

Είμαι απογοητευμένος/η (Íme apogoitevménos/i)

Pronunciation: EE-meh ah-poh-goh-ee-tev-MEH-nohs/nee

Meaning: "I'm disappointed" or "I'm frustrated"

When to use it: To express your emotional state. Use masculine (-ος) or feminine (-η) ending.

Με ενοχλεί όταν... (Me enohli ótan)

Pronunciation: meh eh-noh-HLEE OH-tahn

Meaning: "It bothers me when..."

When to use it: To express specific behaviors that upset you.

Example: "Με ενοχλεί όταν αργείς χωρίς να με ειδοποιήσεις." (It bothers me when you're late without letting me know.)

Χρειάζομαι λίγο χρόνο (Hriázome lígo hróno)

Pronunciation: hree-AH-zoh-meh LEE-goh HROH-noh

Meaning: "I need a little time"

When to use it: When you need to step back and calm down.

Αυτό με πληγώνει (Aftó me pligóni)

Pronunciation: ahf-TOH meh plee-GOH-nee

Meaning: "That hurts me"

When to use it: To express emotional pain from your partner's words.

Setting Boundaries

Establishing limits is a vital part of any healthy communication, especially during heated exchanges where emotions might lead to disrespectful behavior. In Greek, setting a firm boundary often starts with the way you are being addressed. The phrase "Don't speak to me like that" (Μη μου μιλάς έτσι) acts as a direct intervention against an inappropriate tone or language, ensuring that the dialogue remains respectful regardless of the topic.

Beyond tone, it is important to recognize when the content of an argument has become unrealistic or dramatic. Calling something "too much" or "excessive" (Αυτό είναι υπερβολικό) helps ground the exchange in reality. To bring the temperature down, you can propose that the conversation continues only if it can be done in a composed manner (Πρέπει να μιλήσουμε ήρεμα), which prioritizes rational discussion over reactive outbursts.

Ήρεμα Calmly / Quietly

Pronunciation: EE-re-ma

"Μίλα μου πιο ήρεμα, σε παρακαλώ."

Μη μου μιλάς έτσι (Mi mou milás étsi)

Pronunciation: mee moo mee-LAHS EHT-see

Meaning: "Don't talk to me like that"

When to use it: When the tone becomes disrespectful.

Αυτό είναι υπερβολικό (Aftó íne ipervolikó)

Pronunciation: ahf-TOH EE-neh ee-pehr-voh-lee-KOH

Meaning: "That's excessive" or "That's too much"

When to use it: When something crosses a line.

Πρέπει να μιλήσουμε ήρεμα (Prépi na milísoume írema)

Pronunciation: PREH-pee nah mee-LEE-soo-meh EE-reh-mah

Meaning: "We need to talk calmly"

When to use it: To de-escalate a heated argument.

Seeking Resolution

The final stage of any disagreement should focus on moving forward and finding common ground. Transitioning into this phase requires a shift from defensive language to collaborative inquiry. Asking "What can we do?" (Τι μπορούμε να κάνουμε?) or suggesting that you find a solution together (Μπορούμε να βρούμε λύση μαζί?) invites the other person to participate in the healing process rather than remaining an opponent.

Active empathy is one of the most effective tools for de-escalation in Greek. Expressing a genuine desire to see things from the other person's perspective (Θέλω να σε καταλάβω) can soften a tense atmosphere and build trust. These phrases serve as a bridge between the conflict and a productive outcome, ensuring that the relationship remains intact after the disagreement is settled.

Λύση Solution

Pronunciation: LEE-see

"Πρέπει να βρούμε μια λύση για το πρόβλημα."

Μπορούμε να βρούμε λύση μαζί; (Boroúme na vroúme lísi mazí?)

Pronunciation: boh-ROO-meh nah VROO-meh LEE-see mah-ZEE

Meaning: "Can we find a solution together?"

When to use it: When ready to work toward resolution.

Θέλω να σε καταλάβω (Thélo na se katalávo)

Pronunciation: THEH-loh nah seh kah-tah-LAH-voh

Meaning: "I want to understand you"

When to use it: To show genuine interest in your partner's perspective.

Τι μπορούμε να κάνουμε; (Ti boroúme na kánoume?)

Pronunciation: tee boh-ROO-meh nah KAH-noo-meh

Meaning: "What can we do?"

When to use it: To shift from arguing to problem-solving.

Cultural Insights

Greek communication is famously expressive. Raised voices, animated gestures, and passionate exchanges are normal—they show engagement, not necessarily crisis. Don't interpret intensity as danger; it's often just how Greeks communicate.

However, respect matters deeply. Avoid saying anything that could dishonor your partner or their familyf="/learn/en/el/greek-video-call-phrases-family/">family.

After arguments, warmth returns quickly in Greek culture. Sharing food, physical affection, and expressions of love restore harmony. "Σ'αγαπώ" (S'agapó - I love you) or "Συγγνώμη" (Signómi - Sorryef="/learn/en/el/el-forgive-phrases-for-english-speakers/">Sorry) carry deep meaning.

Practice Makes Progress

Learning these phrases shows your Greek partner you're committed to understanding them fully. Practice during calm times so they're ready when emotions run high. Your effort to navigate difficult/learn/en/el/greek-support-phrases-for-english-speakers/">difficulties in Greek strengthens your bond.

title: Greek Baby & Pregnancy Vocabulary: Essential Words for Expecting Couples
content_html:
Expecting a μωρό with your Greek-speaking partner is an exciting journey. Learning the essential μωρό and pregnancy vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-restaurant-vocabulary-dining-out/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-moving-in-together/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-honeymoon-travel-phrases/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-expressing-emotions-feelings-vocabulary/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-date-night-vocabulary/">vocabulary in Greek will help you participate fully in doctor visits, shopping for the παιδικό δωμάτιο, and those precious first conversations with your little one.

For everyday communication with your partner, check out our guide on essential Greek phrases for couples.

💕

Phrase to Learn

μωρό

baby

[ mo-RO ]

The most important word for your growing family.

Essential Pregnancy Vocabulary

έγκυος pregnant

Pronunciation: EHNG-kee-oss

"Είναι έγκυος και περιμένουμε το μωρό μας."

Greek English Context
μωρό baby Your little one
έγκυος pregnant The condition
περιμένω παιδί expecting Anticipating baby

Greek Pregnancy Traditions

In Greek-speaking cultures, pregnancy is often celebrated with specific traditions. Baby showers, gender reveals, and family gatherings are common ways to welcome the new arrival. It's traditional to wait until after birth to buy certain items.

Announcing the News

Sharing the news that you're περιμένω παιδί a μωρό represents one of life's most joyful moments. In Greek-speaking cultures, family plays a central role in pregnancy announcements. Consider telling your partner's parents="/learn/en/el/greek-video-call-phrases-family/">parents in person rather than via text or phone—this shows respect for cultural values around family hierarchy.

The timing of your announcement matters culturally. Many Greek families prefer waiting until after the first trimester before sharing widely. This tradition stems from both medical caution and cultural superstition. Discuss with your partner when feels right for their family's expectations.

Your announcement style can reflect your relationship's unique blend of cultures. Mixing English with Greek words like "μωρό" and "περιμένω παιδί" creates an authentic message that honors both linguistic backgrounds. Family members will appreciate the effort you're making to incorporate Greek into this milestone.

Expect emotional reactions. Greek culture encourages open displays of joy, tears, and excitement. What might seem like an overwhelming response is actually a sign of how much your news means to the family. Extended family members may immediately start planning celebrations and offering advice.

To Your Partner's Family

  • We're περιμένω παιδί a μωρό! - The big announcement
  • You're going to be grandparents! - For the parents
  • We have wonderful news! - Building anticipation

To Friends

  • We're having a μωρό! - Direct and joyful
  • Our family is growing! - Gentle announcement
  • Little one on the way! - Cute phrasing

At the Doctor's Office

Medical Appointments

In Greek-speaking healthcare settings, prenatal care may follow specific schedules. Having your partner help translate during appointments is invaluable, or bring a translation app as backup.

Nursery and Baby Items

Shopping for baby items with your Greek-speaking partner offers wonderful opportunities to learn practical vocabulary while making important decisions together. Each purchase represents preparation for your μωρό's arrival.

Greek-speaking cultures may have specific preferences for certain baby items. Some families strongly prefer particular brands or styles based on what they used for previous generations. Rather than viewing these preferences as limitations, see them as connections to family traditions your child will inherit.

The vocabulary for baby items differs between casual conversation and formal product labels. In stores, you'll encounter both Greek terms and transliterated English words. "Μπιμπερό" comes from "baby bottle" but is fully integrated into Greek. Understanding these linguistic borrowings helps you navigate shopping more easily.

Safety standards and availability vary across countries. When discussing baby items with Greek-speaking family members, be aware that recommendations may reflect products available in Greece rather than where you live. This cultural difference requires respectful negotiation about what works for your specific situation.

title: Greek Birthday Wishes for Your Partner: Sweet Messages That Touch the Heart
content_html:
Your partner's γενέθλια is the perfect opportunity to show your love in Greek. Whether you're writing a heartfelt card, planning a έκπληξη πάρτι, or simply want to make them feel ξεχωριστός, these phrases will help you express your feelings beautifully.

For more ways to express affection, check out our guide on Greek compliments for your partner.

💕

Phrase to Learn

Να ζήσεις!

May you live long!

[ nah ZEE-sees ]

The essential greeting for your partner's special day.

Essential Birthday Vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-restaurant-vocabulary-dining-out/">Vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-moving-in-together/">Vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-honeymoon-travel-phrases/">Vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-expressing-emotions-feelings-vocabulary/">Vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-date-night-vocabulary/">Vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-baby-pregnancy-vocabulary/">Vocabulary

γιορτή celebration / feast day

Pronunciation: yee-or-TEE

"Η γιορτή των γενεθλίων σου είναι πάντα ξεχωριστή."

Greek English Usage
γενέθλια birthday The special day
χρόνια πολλά happy birthday The greeting
ευχή wish Birthday desires
γιορτάζω celebrate To honor the day
δώρο gift Birthday present
τούρτα cake Birthday tradition
κεριά candles On the cake
πάρτι party Celebration
έκπληξη surprise Unexpected joy
ξεχωριστός special Extraordinary
🇬🇷

Greek Birthday Traditions

In Greek-speaking cultures, birthdays often include singing traditional songs, making wishes while blowing out κεριά, and sharing τούρτα with loved ones. The person celebrating may also be expected to bring treats for others.

Sweet Birthday Messages

Greek birthday greetings/learn/en/el/greek-good-morning-goodnight-phrases/">greetings often start with the foundational phrase "Χρόνια Πολλά" (Many Years), but when addressing a partner, adding a layer of tenderness distinguishes a casual greeting from a romantic one. This section explores how to balance traditional well-wishes with intimate adjectives that reflect a deep personal connection. Whether you require Simple Wishes that get straight to the point or Romantic Messages that delve into more poetic expressions of devotion, these phrases provide the necessary linguistic tools.

Understanding the nuance of Greek terms of endearment is essential for these messages. In the following examples, focus is placed on the use of the second person singular to maintain a sense of closeness. The vocabulary emphasizes companionship and shared future goals, moving beyond the standard celebratory clichés used for acquaintances or distant relatives.

καρδιά μου my heart (term of endearment)

Pronunciation: kar-THYA moo

"Χρόνια πολλά, καρδιά μου, σ'αγαπώ!"

Simple Wishes

  • χρόνια πολλά, my love! - Classic and heartfelt
  • Wishing you all the best! - Traditional well-wishes
  • May all your ευχήes come true! - Magical sentiment

Romantic Messages

  • You make my life ξεχωριστός - Expressing gratitude
  • Every day with you is a δώρο - Love declaration
  • You are the best έκπληξη in my life - Romantic twist
μοναδικός/μοναδική unique / one of a kind (m/f)

Pronunciation: moh-nah-thee-KOS / moh-nah-thee-KEE

"Για μένα είσαι ο πιο μοναδικός άνθρωπος στον κόσμο."

Birthday Card Messages

Putting pen to paper in a foreign language requires a different level of precision than spoken conversation. In Greek culture, birthday cards are frequently kept as mementos, making the choice of words particularly significant for a lasting impression. This section is divided into Heartfelt Short Messages for those who prefer brevity and Longer Romantic Messages for writers who wish to compose a more substantial tribute to their partner.

Greek written well-wishes often incorporate themes of health, longevity, and prosperity. When selecting a message, pay attention to the flow of the sentences and the use of evocative imagery that characterizes Greek romantic prose. By using these templates, you can construct a message that feels both culturally authentic and personally meaningful, ensuring the written sentiment resonates during the celebration.

ευτυχία happiness

Pronunciation: ef-tee-KHEE-a

"Σου εύχομαι όλη την ευτυχία του κόσμου."

Heartfelt Short Messages

"χρόνια πολλά to the most ξεχωριστός person in my world!"

"On your γενέθλια, I want you to know how much you mean to me."

Longer Romantic Messages

"Every γενέθλια I get to γιορτάζω with you is a blessing. You make my life complete. χρόνια πολλά, my love!"

"Another year of loving you is my greatest δώρο. May this γενέθλια bring you all the joy you deserve."

🇬🇷

Birthday Gift Etiquette

In Greek-speaking cultures, δώροs are usually opened immediately when received. It's customary to express genuine enthusiasm and gratitude. Practical and thoughtful δώροs are often valued over extravagant ones.

Planning the Perfect Birthday

Organizing a celebration in a Greek context involves specific terminology related to hospitality and coordination. To successfully navigate the logistics of a gathering, one must become familiar with Party Vocabulary that covers everything from the venue to the menu. Mastering these terms allows for smoother communication with vendors or friends who are assisting with the birthday arrangements.

If the objective is to catch a partner off guard with a celebration, the Surprise Party Phrases provided below offer the necessary discreet language to keep the event a secret. From whispered instructions to the final exclamation of discovery, these phrases help maintain the element of surprise while ensuring everyone involved in the planning stays coordinated in their efforts.

δώρο gift / present

Pronunciation: THO-roh

"Σου έχω ένα μικρό δώρο για τα γενέθλιά σου."

Party Vocabulary

Greek English Context

Surprise Party Phrases

  • έκπληξη! - When they walk in
  • We're here to γιορτάζω! - Explaining the party
  • Make a ευχή! - Before blowing candles

Birthday Morning Messages

Singing Happy Birthday

Learn to sing the γενέθλια song in Greek! This is one of the most touching ways to honor your partner's culture on their ξεχωριστός day.

χρονιά year

Pronunciation: khroh-NYA

"Εύχομαι αυτή η χρονιά να σου φέρει πολλά χαμόγελα."

Birthday Milestone Messages

Reaching a new decade is viewed as a significant transition in Greek society, representing a shift in wisdom and life experience. These milestones provide an opportunity to reflect on past achievements while looking forward to future endeavors with a partner. This section breaks down specific greetings for the 30th Birthday, 40th Birthday, and 50th Birthday, acknowledging the unique cultural weight each age carries.

Each milestone has its own linguistic nuance; for instance, the thirtieth birthday often focuses on blooming and personal growth, while the fiftieth emphasizes a "golden" era of life and stability. Using age-specific vocabulary demonstrates a high level of cultural fluency and recognizes the gravity of the partner's specific life stage through appropriate idiomatic expressions.

ευγνωμοσύνη gratitude

Pronunciation: ef-ghno-moh-SEE-nee

"Νιώθω μεγάλη ευγνωμοσύνη που σε έχω στη ζωή μου."

30th Birthday

"Welcome to your thirties! The best γενέθλια for the best partner!"

40th Birthday

"Forty years of amazing you! Here's to γιορτάζωing many more!"

Making your partner feel loved on their γενέθλια in their native Greek creates memories that last a lifetime. Every word spoken with love becomes a δώρο in itself.

title: 50 Greek Compliments for Your Partner
content_html:
Greeks are known for their passionate and expressive nature, and compliments play a central role in Greek romance. Learning how to compliment your Greek partner in their native language will not only impress them but also show the depth of your commitment to understanding their culture.

In this guide, you will discover beautiful Greek compliments for every occasion - from everyday sweetness to deeply romantic expressions that will make your partner's heart soar.

Physical Compliments

Greeks appreciate beauty and are not shy about expressing admiration. Here are compliments for your partner's appearance:

For Her

Είσαι το φως μου You are my light

Pronunciation: EE-seh toh FOS moo

"Είσαι το φως μου, φωτίζεις τη ζωή μου. (You are my light, you light up my life.)"

Με εμπνέεις You inspire me

Pronunciation: meh em-PNEH-ees

"Με εμπνέεις κάθε μέρα με τη δύναμή σου. (You inspire me every day with your strength.)"

Είσαι η ψυχή μου You are my soul

Pronunciation: EE-seh ee psee-HEE moo

"Είσαι η ψυχή μου, δεν μπορώ να φανταστώ τη ζωή χωρίς εσένα. (You are my soul, I can't imagine life without you.)"

For Him

Είσαι ο φύλακας άγγελός μου You are my guardian angel

Pronunciation: EE-seh oh FEE-lah-kas ANG-geh-LOS moo

"Είσαι ο φύλακας άγγελός μου, με προστατεύεις πάντα. (You are my guardian angel, you always protect me.)"

Έχεις υπέροχη προσωπικότητα You have a wonderful personality

Pronunciation: EH-hees ee-PEH-roh-hee pro-so-pee-koh-TEE-tah

"Έχεις υπέροχη προσωπικότητα, είσαι μοναδικός/ή. (You have a wonderful personality, you are unique.)"

Είσαι το άλλο μου μισό You are my other half

Pronunciation: EE-seh toh AH-loh moo mee-SOH

"Είσαι το άλλο μου μισό, με συμπληρώνεις. (You are my other half, you complete me.)"

🇬🇷

Greek Expressiveness

Greeks are naturally expressive and appreciate enthusiastic compliments. Do not hold back! Words like "πανέμορφη" (gorgeous) or "θεά" (goddess) may seem over the top in English, but in Greek culture, such expressions are common and deeply appreciated.

Specific Feature Compliments

Η φωνή σου είναι μελωδία Your voice is a melody

Pronunciation: ee foh-NEE soo EE-neh meh-loh-THEE-ah

"Η φωνή σου είναι μελωδία στα αυτιά μου. (Your voice is a melody in my ears.)"

Το άγγιγμά σου είναι μαγικό Your touch is magical

Pronunciation: toh ANG-gee-GMAH soo EE-neh mah-yee-KOH

"Το άγγιγμά σου είναι μαγικό, με κάνει να νιώθω ασφαλής. (Your touch is magical, it makes me feel safe.)"

Λατρεύω το γέλιο σου I love your laugh

Pronunciation: lah-TREV-oh toh YEH-lyoh soo

"Λατρεύω το γέλιο σου, είναι μεταδοτικό. (I love your laugh, it's contagious.)"

Είσαι τόσο ευγενικός/ή You are so kind (m/f)

Pronunciation: EE-seh TOH-soh ef-yeh-nee-KOS / ef-yeh-nee-KEE

"Είσαι τόσο ευγενικός/ή με όλους. (You are so kind to everyone.)"

💕

Phrase to Learn

Σ' αγαπώ πιο πολύ από οτιδήποτε

I love you more than anything

[ sah-ghah-POH pyoh poh-LEE ah-Poh oh-tee-DEE-poh-teh ]

Use this when your partner looks absolutely stunning

Personality Compliments

Complimenting personality shows deeper appreciation:

Είσαι το στήριγμά μου You are my support

Pronunciation: EE-seh toh STEE-reeg-MAH moo

"Είσαι το στήριγμά μου σε όλα τα δύσκολα. (You are my support through all difficult/learn/en/el/greek-support-phrases-for-english-speakers/">difficulties.)"

Είσαι ο καλύτερός μου φίλος/η You are my best friend (m/f)

Pronunciation: EE-seh oh kah-lee-TEH-ros moo FEE-los / ee kah-lee-TEH-ree moo FEE-lee

"Είσαι ο καλύτερός μου φίλος/η και ο σύντροφός μου. (You are my best friend and my partner.)"

Έχεις χρυσή καρδιά You have a heart of gold

Pronunciation: EH-hees khree-SEE kar-THYAH

"Έχεις χρυσή καρδιά, πάντα βοηθάς τους άλλους. (You have a heart of gold, you always help others.)"

Είσαι ο μόνος/η για μένα You are the only one for me (m/f)

Pronunciation: EE-seh oh MOH-nos / ee MOH-nee yah MEH-nah

"Είσαι ο μόνος/η για μένα, κανείς άλλος δεν με καταλαβαίνει έτσι. (You are the only one for me, no one else understands me like this.)"

Character Traits

Greek English Pronunciation
Είσαι τόσο γενναιόδωρη/ος You are so generous EE-seh TOH-soh yeh-neh-OH-thoh-ree/ros
Είσαι πολύ αστεία/αστείος You are very funny EE-seh poh-LEE ah-STEE-ah / ah-STEE-os
Είσαι τόσο δυνατή/δυνατός You are so strong EE-seh TOH-soh thee-nah-TEE / thee-nah-TOS
Είσαι πολύ ταλαντούχα/ος You are very talented EE-seh poh-LEE tah-lan-DOO-hah/hos
🇬🇷

Balancing Compliments

While Greeks love compliments, they also value sincerity. The best compliments come from genuine observation. Notice what makes your partner special - their laugh, their kindness, their creativity - and express it authentically.

Romantic and Affectionate Compliments

These phrases express deep romantic feelings:

Κάνεις τη ζωή μου καλύτερη You make my life better

Pronunciation: KAH-nees tee zoh-EE moo kah-LEE-teh-ree

"Κάνεις τη ζωή μου καλύτερη με την παρουσία σου. (You make my life better with your presence.)"

Με κάνεις να νιώθω ολοκληρωμένος/η You make me feel complete (m/f)

Pronunciation: meh KAH-nees nah NYOH-thoh oh-loh-klee-roh-MEH-nos / oh-loh-klee-roh-MEH-nee

"Με κάνεις να νιώθω ολοκληρωμένος/η. (You make me feel complete.)"

Είσαι το όνειρό μου που έγινε πραγματικότητα You are my dream come true

Pronunciation: EE-seh toh OH-nee-ROH moo poo EH-yee-neh prah-gmah-tee-KOH-tee-tah

"Είσαι το όνειρό μου που έγινε πραγματικότητα. (You are my dream come true.)"

Είσαι το λιμάνι μου You are my haven / port

Pronunciation: EE-seh toh lee-MAH-nee moo

"Είσαι το λιμάνι μου, εκεί που νιώθω ασφαλής. (You are my haven, where I feel safe.)"

Compliments About Actions

Λύνεις προβλήματα με ευκολία You solve problems with ease

Pronunciation: LEE-nees pro-VLEE-mah-tah meh ef-koh-LEE-ah

"Λύνεις προβλήματα με ευκολία, είσαι πολύ αποτελεσματικός/ή. (You solve problems with ease, you are very effective.)"

Η αφοσίωσή σου είναι αξιοθαύμαστη Your dedication is admirable

Pronunciation: ee ah-foh-SYOH-see soo EE-neh ak-see-oh-THAV-mah-stee

"Η αφοσίωσή σου είναι αξιοθαύμαστη σε ό,τι κάνεις. (Your dedication is admirable in everything you do.)"

Έχεις υπέροχη αίσθηση του χιούμορ You have a great sense of humor

Pronunciation: EH-hees ee-PEH-roh-hee EES-thee-see too HYOO-mor

"Έχεις υπέροχη αίσθηση του χιούμορ, με κάνεις πάντα να γελάω. (You have a great sense of humor, you always make me laugh.)"

Compliments for Special Moments

Relationships thrive on the recognition of the extraordinary within the ordinary. While casual praise is essential, special occasions and acts of kindness require more nuanced Greek vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-restaurant-vocabulary-dining-out/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-moving-in-together/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-honeymoon-travel-phrases/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-expressing-emotions-feelings-vocabulary/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-date-night-vocabulary/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-baby-pregnancy-vocabulary/">vocabulary to convey deep admiration. Using specific adjectives helps bridge the gap between simple acknowledgment and heartfelt validation.

The following phrases are categorized to help you navigate two distinct scenarios: when your partner makes a visible effort to look their best and when they provide much-needed support. You will find terms specifically for when they dress up for an evening out, as well as expressions designed to offer genuine gratitude after they help you with a task or emotional burden.

χαριτωμένος/η charming/cute (m/f)

Pronunciation: hah-ree-toh-MEH-nos / hah-ree-toh-MEH-nee

"Είσαι τόσο χαριτωμένος/η όταν κοιμάσαι. (You are so cute when you sleep.)"

When They Dress Up

Είσαι λαμπερός/ή You are radiant (m/f)

Pronunciation: EE-seh lah-mpeh-ROS / lah-mpeh-REE

"Είσαι λαμπερός/ή ακόμα και το πρωί. (You are radiant even in the morning.)"

Ο τρόπος που με κοιτάς The way you look at me

Pronunciation: oh TRO-pos poo meh koh-TAHS

"Ο τρόπος που με κοιτάς, με κάνει να λιώνω. (The way you look at me makes me melt.)"

After They Help You

Με κάνεις να νιώθω ασφαλής You make me feel safe

Pronunciation: meh KAH-nees nah NYOH-thoh ah-sfah-LEES

"Με κάνεις να νιώθω ασφαλής όταν είμαι μαζί σου. (You make me feel safe when I'm with you.)"

Είσαι το πιο σημαντικό πρόσωπο You are the most important person

Pronunciation: EE-seh toh pyoh see-mahn-tee-KOH PRO-so-poh

"Είσαι το πιο σημαντικό πρόσωπο στη ζωή μου. (You are the most important person in my life.)"

The Verb "To Be" for Compliments

Master the verb "είμαι" (to be) for constructing compliments:

Είμαι

to be

I είμαι I am
You (singular) είσαι you are
He/She/It είναι he/she/it is
We είμαστε we are
You (plural) είστε you are
They είναι they are

Useful Adjectives for Compliments

Build your compliment vocabulary with these essential adjectives:

Greek (f/m) English Pronunciation
υπέροχη/υπέροχος wonderful ee-PEH-roh-hee / ee-PEH-roh-hos
καταπληκτική/καταπληκτικός amazing kah-tah-pleek-tee-KEE / kah-tah-pleek-tee-KOS
μοναδική/μοναδικός unique moh-nah-thee-KEE / moh-nah-thee-KOS
τέλεια/τέλειος perfect TEH-lyah / TEH-lyos
αξιαγάπητη/αξιαγάπητος adorable, lovable ak-see-ah-GHAH-pee-tee / ak-see-ah-GHAH-pee-tos
γλυκιά/γλυκός sweet ghlee-KYAH / ghlee-KOS
🇬🇷

Gender Agreement

Greek adjectives change based on the gender of the person you are describing. Most adjectives end in -η/-ή for feminine and -ος for masculine. This guide shows both forms (f/m) when applicable.

Practice Exercises for Couples

Transitioning from memorizing a list of phrases to using them naturally in conversation requires intentionality. These structured activities move the vocabulary from the page into your daily interactions, helping the Greek language become a permanent part of your shared communication style. Each method focuses on a different sensory experience to help the brain retain linguistic patterns more effectively.

To solidify these new terms, we have outlined three specific methods: a daily compliment challenge to build consistency, a guide for creating physical compliment cards for a tactile experience, and a mirror exercise to help you master pronunciation and facial expressions. Each approach targets a different aspect of language acquisition, from auditory recall to written reinforcement.

ευτυχία happiness

Pronunciation: ef-tee-HEE-a

"Η ευτυχία μου είναι να είμαι μαζί σου. (My happiness is to be with you.)"

Daily Compliment Challenge

Take turns giving each other one Greek compliment each day. Try to use different adjectives and notice how your partner reacts to each one.

Compliment Cards

Write Greek compliments on small cards and hide them for your partner to find throughout the day:

  • Είσαι η χαρά της ζωής μου (You are the joy of my life)
  • Σ'αγαπώ πιο πολύ κάθε μέρα (I love you more every day)
  • Είσαι υπέροχη/υπέροχος (You are wonderful)

Mirror Exercise

Look into each other's eyes and take turns giving sincere compliments in Greek. This builds both vocabulary and emotional connection.

Quick Reference Card

Situation Greek English
General beauty Είσαι πανέμορφη/πανέμορφος You are gorgeous
Their outfit Σου πάει πολύ! It suits you well!
Their smile Έχεις υπέροχο χαμόγελο You have a wonderful smile
Their personality Είσαι απίστευτη/απίστευτος You are incredible
After help Είσαι η/ο καλύτερη/ος You are the best
Deep affection Είσαι τα πάντα μου You are my everything
Stunning moment Μου κόβεις την ανάσα You take my breath away

Compliments are the currency of romance in Greek culture. When you tell your partner "Είσαι πανέμορφη" or "Μου κόβεις την ανάσα," you are speaking the language of their heart.

Practice these phrases often, say them with genuine feeling, and watch your partner light up every time you express your admiration in Greek.

title: 50 Greek Date Night Vocabulary Words to Impress Your Partner
content_html:
Planning a romantic evening with your Greek partner? Nothing melts hearts faster than whispering sweet Greek words during date night. These 50 essential vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-honeymoon-travel-phrases/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-expressing-emotions-feelings-vocabulary/">vocabularylearn/en/el/greek-baby-pregnancy-vocabulary/">vocabulary words will transform your evenings together, from making restaurant reservations to sharing those intimate moments that make your connection even stronger.

Phrase to Learn

Ραντεβού

Date

[ rahn-deh-VOO ]

Perfect for asking your partner out - 'Θέλεις να βγούμε ραντεβού απόψε;'

The Essential 10: Words for Every Date Night

These ten words form the backbone of any romantic Greek evening together. Practice them with your partner and watch how quickly your conversations become more intimate.

Βράδυ Evening

Pronunciation: VRAH-dee

"Τι όμορφο βράδυ για έξοδο!"

Greek English Pronunciation When to Use

Greek Dating Culture

Greeks often date in groups initially, so don't be surprised if friends join early dates. Private one-on-one dates become more common as relationships develop.

Restaurant Romance: Dining Out Together

Greek cuisine is central to dating culture. These words will help you navigate any taverna or restaurant with confidence.

Μερίδα Portion

Pronunciation: meh-REE-thah

"Μία μερίδα μουσακά για δύο, παρακαλώ."

Menu Must-Knows

Greek English Pronunciation
Ορεκτικό Appetizer oh-rehk-tee-KOH
Κυρίως πιάτο Main course kee-REE-ohs PEE-ah-toh

Sweet Compliments for Your Date

Learn these romantic phrases to make your partner feel special during dinner. For more heartfelt expressions, explore our Greek compliments for your partner.

Greek English Pronunciation
Όμορφη Beautiful (feminine) OH-mor-fee

Evening Activities: Making Plans Together

Entertainment Essentials

Σινεμά Cinema/movies

Romantic Gestures: Sweet Nothings

These tender words will make your Greek partner's heart flutter. Pair them with Greek pet names for your partner for extra romance.

Greek Physical Affection

Greeks are naturally expressive and affectionate. Holding hands, gentle touches on the arm, and warm greetings/learn/en/el/greek-good-morning-goodnight-phrases/">greetings are normal in Greek culture - don't be surprised by the warmth!

Practical Date Night Phrases

These essential phrasesn/el/essential-greek-phrases-for-couples/">essential phrases will help you navigate any date situation smoothly.

Making Plans

Προτείνω I suggest
